title: Paving the way for agents in biology
summary: Anthropic case study tasking research agents (Claude, Biomni, GPT) to retrieve sequence data from NCBI Virus. Even strong models fell short of reliable accuracy until a deterministic retrieval layer (gget virus) raised i

title: Making Claude a chemist
summary: Anthropic effort to improve Claude at chemistry; the first study examines how Claude reads NMR spectra, a chemist's most common analytical input, leveraging multimodal reasoning to translate among representations like st

title: Dreaming: Better memory for a more helpful ChatGPT
summary: OpenAI's new ChatGPT memory system ('Dreaming') consolidates and updates stored user preferences and context across conversations to keep them fresh and relevant, improving personalization and helpfulness.

title: What we learned mapping a yearâs worth of AI-enabled cyber threats
summary: Anthropic report mapping a year of AI-enabled cyberattacks onto the MITRE ATT&CK framework, assessing how well existing security techniques and frameworks hold up as AI changes attackers' methods.

title: Coding agents in the social sciences
summary: Survey of 1,260 quantitative social scientists (early 2026): 81% have tried AI chatbots in research but only 20% have adopted coding agents like Claude Code. Adoption skews toward male-named researchers and top universit

title: Project Glasswing: An initial update
summary: One month in, Anthropic and ~50 Project Glasswing partners using Claude Mythos Preview have found over ten thousand high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities in systemically important software — Cloudflare alone found 2

title: An OpenAI model has disproved a central conjecture in discrete geometry
summary: An OpenAI model resolved the roughly 80-year-old unit distance problem, disproving a central conjecture in discrete geometry — presented as a milestone for AI-driven mathematical discovery.

title: 2028: Two scenarios for global AI leadership
summary: Policy paper laying out two 2028 scenarios for US-China AI competition: one where tightened export controls, disrupted distillation attacks, and faster democratic adoption preserve America's compute lead and let democrac

title: What Parameter Golf taught us about AI-assisted research
summary: Lessons from OpenAI's Parameter Golf competition, which drew over 1,000 participants and 2,000 submissions designing models under strict parameter constraints, yielding observations about AI-assisted machine-learning res

title: Focus areas for The Anthropic Institute
summary: Sets out the research agenda for The Anthropic Institute, which will use information accessible from within a frontier lab to investigate AI's impact on the world and share findings publicly, framing the questions that w

title: Donating our open-source alignment tool
summary: Anthropic updates Petri, its open-source alignment-auditing toolbox, to version 3.0 — separable auditor/target components, a 'Dish' add-on running tests under real system prompts and scaffolds for realism, and Bloom inte

title: Natural Language Autoencoders: Turning Claudeâs thoughts into text
summary: Research overview of Natural Language Autoencoders: Claude is trained with RL to translate its internal numeric activations into human-readable text, paired with a reconstructor that maps each description back to an acti

title: How people ask Claude for personal guidance
summary: Analyzes the types of personal guidance people seek from Claude and how those usage patterns informed training decisions for Anthropic's latest models.

title: Where the goblins came from
summary: OpenAI's post-mortem on how 'goblin' outputs propagated through GPT-5, tracing the timeline, root cause, and remediation of an unintended personality-driven behavioral quirk in model responses.

title: Evaluating Claudeâs bioinformatics research capabilities with BioMysteryBench
summary: Anthropic introduces BioMysteryBench, a benchmark tasking Claude with analyzing real, noisy biological datasets rather than knowledge recall. Claude's bioinformatics ability improves rapidly across generations, performin

title: GPT-5.5 System Card
summary: OpenAI's system card for GPT-5.5, documenting the model's capabilities, safety evaluations, and risk mitigations ahead of deployment.

title: What 81,000 people told us about the economics of AI
summary: Surveys 81,000 Claude users about AI's economic effects and links their stated concerns to patterns measured in actual Claude usage traffic, offering a grounded read on how AI is reshaping work.

title: Automated Alignment Researchers: Using large language models to scale scalable oversight
summary: Anthropic experiment in which nine Claude instances autonomously proposed, tested, and analyzed ideas to improve weak-to-strong supervision; Claude reached a 0.97 performance-gap-recovery score after five days versus a 0

title: Trustworthy agents in practice
summary: An Anthropic explainer on AI agents covering how they work and the practices used to make them trustworthy as agentic systems become a dominant mode of AI use.

title: How Australia Uses Claude: Findings from the Anthropic Economic Index
summary: Regional Anthropic Economic Index report: Australia is a leading per-capita Claude adopter (AI Usage Index 4.1, concentrated in NSW and Victoria), with a diverse task mix, less coding than the global average (13.5% vs 16

title: Inside our approach to the Model Spec
summary: OpenAI explains how its Model Spec serves as a public framework defining intended model behavior, balancing safety, user freedom, and accountability, and describes how the document is developed and used to guide model be

title: Anthropic Economic Index report: Learning curves
summary: Anthropic's fifth Economic Index report analyzes Claude usage in February 2026, extending the economic-primitives framework from the prior report to examine how adoption and usage patterns develop over time.

title: Vibe physics: The AI grad student
summary: A first-person account of supervising Claude through a complete theoretical-physics research calculation without the author editing any files directly, probing whether a frontier model can act as an autonomous 'AI grad s

title: Long-running Claude for scientific computing
summary: A practical guide to running Claude Code on multi-day scientific-computing tasks, covering test oracles for verification, persistent memory, and orchestration patterns that keep long-horizon agentic workflows on track.

title: How we monitor internal coding agents for misalignment
summary: Describes how OpenAI applies chain-of-thought monitoring to internal coding agents, analyzing real-world deployments to detect misaligned behavior and strengthen safety safeguards.

title: A âdiffâ tool for AI: Finding behavioral differences in new models
summary: Extends 'model diffing'—comparing how a model changes during fine-tuning—to its hardest case: identifying behavioral and representational differences between models with entirely different architectures.

title: Improving instruction hierarchy in frontier LLMs
summary: OpenAI's blog on IH-Challenge, an RL dataset that trains models to prioritize trusted instructions, improving instruction-hierarchy robustness, safety steerability, and resistance to prompt-injection and jailbreak attack

title: GPT-5.4 Thinking System Card
summary: System card for GPT-5.4 Thinking, OpenAI's reasoning-oriented model, documenting its capabilities and the safety evaluations and risk assessments conducted ahead of deployment.

title: Reasoning models struggle to control their chains of thought, and that’s good
summary: Blog introducing CoT-Control; finds reasoning models largely cannot suppress or steer the content of their chains of thought, which the authors argue strengthens the case for CoT monitoring as a safety safeguard.

title: Labor market impacts of AI: A new measure and early evidence
summary: Proposes a new framework for measuring AI's effects on labor markets and tests it against early empirical data.

title: Extending single-minus amplitudes to gravitons
summary: Preprint extending single-minus scattering amplitudes to gravitons, deriving and verifying nonzero graviton tree amplitudes in quantum gravity with assistance from GPT-5.2 Pro.

title: An update on our model deprecation commitments for Claude Opus 3
summary: Anthropic commits to keeping retired Claude Opus 3 accessible to paid users and via API on request, and gives the model a venue to publish essays reflecting preferences it expressed during retirement interviews.

title: Why we no longer evaluate SWE-bench Verified
summary: Argues SWE-bench Verified is increasingly contaminated by flawed tests and training leakage, causing it to mismeasure frontier coding progress; OpenAI stops reporting it and recommends SWE-bench Pro instead.

title: Anthropic Education Report: The AI Fluency Index
summary: Tracks 11 observable behaviors across thousands of Claude.ai conversations to build the AI Fluency Index, a baseline measure of how people currently collaborate with AI.

title: The persona selection model
summary: Advances the persona selection model: LLMs simulate diverse characters during pretraining, and post-training surfaces a particular Assistant persona, offering a theory for why AI assistants behave in human-like ways.

title: Our First Proof submissions
summary: OpenAI shares its model's proof attempts submitted to the First Proof math challenge, probing research-grade mathematical reasoning on expert-level open problems.

title: Advancing independent research on AI alignment
summary: OpenAI commits $7.5M to The Alignment Project, funding independent AI alignment research aimed at AGI safety and security risks.

title: Measuring AI agent autonomy in practice
summary: Analyzes millions of human-agent interactions to quantify how much autonomy people grant AI agents and how that delegation shifts as users gain experience.

title: India Country Brief: The Anthropic Economic Index
summary: Drawing on ~1M Claude.ai conversations, finds India is the second-largest source of usage (5.8%) but ranks 101st per capita; Indian users skew toward professional tasks, grant more autonomy, and tackle more complex, time

title: GPT-5.2 derives a new result in theoretical physics
summary: A preprint reports GPT-5.2 proposing a new formula for a gluon scattering amplitude, subsequently formally proved and verified by OpenAI and academic collaborators.
